member.name: T:Lux.IfLetStmt
member.summary: if-let binding: evaluates Init, binds result to Var if truthy, else runs optional Else branch (may be another IfLetStmt, IfStmt, or a plain BlockStmt).

member.name: M:Lux.IfLetStmt.#ctor(System.String,Lux.Expr,Lux.BlockStmt,Lux.Stmt,System.Int32)
member.summary: if-let binding: evaluates Init, binds result to Var if truthy, else runs optional Else branch (may be another IfLetStmt, IfStmt, or a plain BlockStmt).

member.name: T:Lux.ICallable
member.summary: Anything callable from Lux (user functions, native functions, lambdas).

member.name: P:Lux.ICallable.Arity
member.summary: Expected argument count. -1 means variadic (any number of args).

member.name: T:Lux.NativeFunc
member.summary: A built-in function implemented in C# and exposed to Lux scripts.

member.name: T:Lux.LuxFunction
member.summary: A user-defined Lux function with its captured closure environment.

member.name: P:Lux.LuxFunction.Arity
member.summary: Number of declared parameters.

member.name: P:Lux.LuxFunction.Params
member.summary: Declared parameter names in order.

member.name: M:Lux.LuxFunction.Call(Lux.Interpreter,System.Collections.Generic.List{System.Object},Lux.LuxObject)
member.summary: Call the function with the given arguments. If is supplied it is bound to this; otherwise a fresh is created (constructor pattern). Named constructors automatically store themselves as the ctor field on the returned object so reflection can re-invoke them.

member.name: T:Lux.LuxObject
member.summary: A Lux object — a bag of named fields. Fields may be values, functions (methods), or slots that delegate to C# getters/setters. Named constructors automatically inject a ctor field pointing back to the creating function, enabling reflection-based re-construction.

member.name: T:Lux.LiveProperty
T:Lux.ReturnException
member.summary: Thrown internally when a Lux return statement is executed.

member.name: T:Lux.BreakException
member.summary: Thrown internally when a Lux break statement is executed.

member.name: T:Lux.ContinueException
member.summary: Thrown internally when a Lux continue statement is executed.

member.name: T:Lux.LuxThrowException
member.summary: Thrown when a Lux throw statement is executed. Caught by try/catch blocks in Lux scripts; also catchable from C# host code to intercept script-level errors.

member.name: T:Lux.Interpreter
member.summary:
Tree-walking interpreter for the Lux scripting language.
Quick-start:
var interp = new Interpreter(); interp.Register("log", (Action<string>)(msg => Console.WriteLine(msg))); interp.Run(source); double result = interp.CallFunction<double>("myLuxFun", 1, 2);
Built-in functions available to scripts:
print(...) — write to stdout
input() — read a line from stdin
str(v) / num(v) — type conversions
type(v) — runtime type name string
len(v) — length of string, list, or dict
range(start?, end, step?) — numeric range list
assert(cond, msg) — runtime assertion
import(path) — load a .lux file relative to ; returns a namespace object
getType(val) — reflection: returns a type-descriptor object with fields, hasField, getField, setField, getMethod

member.name: P:Lux.Interpreter.BasePath
member.summary: Base directory used to resolve relative paths in import() calls. Set automatically when running a file via ; override if you supply source code directly from a string.

member.name: M:Lux.Interpreter.Define(System.String,System.Object)
member.summary: Define or overwrite a global variable accessible to Lux scripts.

member.name: M:Lux.Interpreter.GetGlobal(System.String)
member.summary: Read the current value of a Lux global variable.

member.name: M:Lux.Interpreter.GetUserGlobalNames
member.summary: Returns the names of all globals defined by user scripts (excludes built-ins such as print, import, getType, etc.). Used by import() to build the exported namespace object.

member.name: T:Lux.LuxBridge
M:Lux.LuxBridge.ToLux(System.Object)
member.summary: Convert any C# value to a Lux-compatible value.

member.name: M:Lux.LuxBridge.FromLux``1(System.Object)
member.summary: Convert a Lux value to a C# type T.

member.name: M:Lux.LuxBridge.FromLux(System.Object,System.Type)
member.summary: Convert a Lux value to a specific C# type (with coercion).

member.name: M:Lux.LuxBridge.WrapLive(System.Object)
member.summary: Like , but properties and public fields are stored as slots so every Lux read/write hits the live C# object. Also covers public fields, which silently skips.

member.name: M:Lux.LuxBridge.RegisterObjectLive(Lux.Interpreter,System.String,System.Object)
member.summary: Register a C# object with fully live property access (read and write).

member.name: M:Lux.LuxBridge.Wrap(System.Object)
M:Lux.LuxBridge.Register(Lux.Interpreter,System.String,System.Delegate)
M:Lux.LuxBridge.RegisterObject(Lux.Interpreter,System.String,System.Object)
M:Lux.LuxBridge.CallFunction(Lux.Interpreter,System.String,System.Object[])
member.summary: Call a named Lux function from C# and get the raw Lux result. var raw = interp.CallFunction("onUpdate", deltaTime);

member.name: M:Lux.LuxBridge.CallFunction``1(Lux.Interpreter,System.String,System.Object[])
member.summary: Call a named Lux function and convert the result to T. double score = interp.CallFunction<double>("getScore");

member.name: M:Lux.LuxBridge.GetGlobal``1(Lux.Interpreter,System.String)
member.summary: Read a global Lux variable and convert it to T.

member.name: M:Lux.LuxBridge.SetGlobal(Lux.Interpreter,System.String,System.Object)
member.summary: Write a C# value into a Lux global variable.

member.name: T:Lux.BridgeTest
member.summary: Demonstrates the C# ↔ Lux bridge API. Run with: lux --bridge-test
